question_text: Which of the following statements about density is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Density tells us how heavy an object is.

options.label: B
options.text: Density is the mass of a substance per unit volume.

options.label: C
options.text: Density increases when an object gets bigger.

options.label: D
options.text: All liquids have the same density as water.

explanation: Density is defined as mass per unit volume. Option A confuses density with mass alone. Option C is incorrect because increasing size doesn't change density. Option D is false because different liquids have different densities (e.g. oil is less dense than water).
